key_points: The lesson 'Kathmandu' by Vikram Seth is a travelogue describing the author's observations during his visit to Nepal’s capital
The author contrasts the chaotic religious rituals at Pashupatinath temple with the calm spiritual atmosphere at Boudhanath stupa
The crowded temple scene reflects the complexity of religious traditions and rituals
The flute seller in the market creates a peaceful musical moment in the middle of the noisy city
The lesson highlights cultural harmony and the universal appeal of music
characters: Narrator – Vikram Seth, who observes the social and cultural life of Kathmandu
Flute seller – Represents artistic simplicity and the calming power of music
Priests and devotees – Participants in the busy religious rituals at the temple
themes: Coexistence of religious traditions in Kathmandu
Contrast between chaos and peace in spiritual environments
Music as a universal cultural expression
literary_devices: Imagery – Detailed descriptions of temple rituals and street scenes
Simile – Flutes compared to the quills of a porcupine
Contrast – Busy temple scene versus peaceful stupa atmosphere
exam_tips: Explain the symbolic meaning of the flute seller’s music
Discuss the contrast between the two religious places
Highlight how the author presents cultural diversity
